name: Loanmax Title Loans
description_short: Loanmax Title Loans at 2596 Cherry Rd, Rock Hill, SC 29732 is Mon-Fri 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 5.0 stars across 727 reviews. Call +1 803-329-1107.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has operated for over 25 years as a title lending company serving customers across multiple states. The company positions itself as helping "hardworking Americans use the equity in their vehicle to access the cash they need." LoanMax operates multiple locations in Nevada, including five in the Las Vegas area and one in Henderson, making it accessible to southern Nevada residents.

The company offers auto title loans (also called car title loans) as their primary product, with maximum loan amounts up to $10,000 per the website. To qualify, customers need only their vehicle, a photo ID, and a clear vehicle title. LoanMax accepts multiple payment methods including cash, check, debit card, and Western Union for loan repayment. The Las Vegas location at 3276 N. Las Vegas Blvd operates Monday-Friday 10 AM-6 PM and Saturday 9 AM-2 PM.

LoanMax distinguishes itself through its multi-state licensing and regulatory compliance, holding specific licenses in Virginia (operated under Fairfax Elite Financial Services), Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, Michigan, and Nevada. The company displays consumer protection disclosures for each state, including Nevada's warning that title loans are for short-term financial needs only. Their physical store presence across Las Vegas provides in-person accessibility, and they offer online account registration and payment options alongside phone support (877-511-CASH).

The honest assessment is that LoanMax operates in the high-cost lending space where title loans typically carry significant interest rates and fees not disclosed on the browsed website. Nevada's own consumer warning on their site indicates title loans should not be used as long-term solutions. While the company provides quick access to cash for those with vehicle equity and limited credit options, customers should carefully evaluate whether a title loan is appropriate given the risk of vehicle repossession if payments are missed.
pros: Multiple Nevada locations (5 in Las Vegas, 1 in Henderson) for convenient access
Large loan amounts available up to $10,000 per application
Quick approval process requiring only vehicle, photo ID, and clear title
Multiple payment methods accepted (cash, check, debit card, Western Union)
Online account registration and payment options available
Licensed and regulated across multiple states with specific state compliance disclosures
Long operating history since 1990 with 25+ years in business
cons: Title loans carry high interest rates and fees (specific rates not disclosed on website)
Risk of vehicle repossession if loan payments are missed or defaulted
Company's own website warns Nevada customers that title loans should only be used for short-term needs, not long-term financial solutions
No mention of credit counseling or alternative products for customers with credit difficulties
best_for: Vehicle owners with immediate cash needs and no access to traditional credit
Consumers with poor credit history who cannot qualify for personal loans or credit cards
People needing emergency funds within 24-48 hours and willing to risk vehicle collateral
diagnosis: LoanMax Title Loans is appropriate for vehicle owners facing genuine financial emergencies who have exhausted other options and can afford the high cost of title lending. The critical caveat is that this is high-risk, high-cost debt: missing payments risks losing your vehicle, and the company's own disclosure warns against using title loans as a long-term solution. Customers should explore payday alternatives, credit counseling, or personal loans before committing to a title loan.
services: Car title loans (vehicles held as collateral)
In-person loan applications at physical locations
Payment acceptance via cash, check, debit card, and Western Union
Multi-state title lending with state-specific licensing and compliance
